mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns when GDP growth remains above 2 percent annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation above 210 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in greater than 55 percent of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 210.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening corporate output capacity and revenue growth, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or zero over consecutive 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 0.5 percent quarterly signals an economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of quarters where GDP growth falls below the 0.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and declining industrial production indicates labor hoarding during an economic contraction, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive forward returns in greater than 50 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 900 thousand units signal a collapsing construction sector that reduces downstream manufacturing demand and financial sector stability, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of periods when housing starts fall below 900 thousand units.
